title: Chapter Forty-Five: Northern Light
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Arrogance was an emotion that encouraged early death.

It was not a feeling to harbour even before eating the fruit. Just because many people treated him as a genius did not mean he should think that way.

*Just give me the fruit. I'll do anything.*

He recalled the deep green pupils set in the Desolate Sect Lord's curved eye-smile. A feeling like falling into an endless abyss. While mentally repeating his plea for the World Tree's fruit, the pavilion door opened.

---

They said it was originally unplanned. The crown prince had personally requested it -- using the pretext of praising the Ming's sword, since he had no justification to meet Lightning Flash privately.

"So you are the blue warriors who were just promoted."

Yeon-shin met the Ming dynasty's third heaven.

An incredibly handsome young master. Ears like precious sword tips -- familiar. Five and a half chi tall, similar to Yeon-shin's own growing height. Under thick jet-black eyebrows, his flashing eyes contained ink-coloured lightning.

Innate dignity in the gaze sweeping over the bowing blue warriors.

Though he had already said they could raise their heads, only Yeon-shin actually did.

*It's not like he'll accumulate merit in my place.*

The emperor himself treated the Desolate Sect Lord as a military king. She had taught martial arts to the current emperor and crown prince both. Since even the crown prince's power could not touch the World Tree's fruit, this was a meaningless meeting to Yeon-shin.

"Having things taken from me is rare," the crown prince said. "Even more so with attention. Yet they say the grand preceptor turned her attention to you. Even I who studied under her felt it." His gaze was direct. "Jealousy arose in my heart."

"I am honoured."

"No. It is my flaw. Something that shouldn't exist in the emperor's crown prince. It is due to my lack of virtue. I wanted to confirm your talent. To dilute this shame a little."

*Force of character.*

Yeon-shin understood. While Wei Ji Myo-hwa of the Zhongnan Sect was called Sword Dragon, this person was sufficient to be called simply Dragon. There was a reason the Ming used the same honorific for both crown prince and heir apparent.

"Could you perform music with a sword?" the crown prince asked.

"...?"

"Many famous swordsmen make sword sounds with one blade. Sword songs are different. It's the realm of true energy control. They say one needs innate sense to turn sword sounds into song."

Silence. Then the crown prince laughed.

"I heard your talent was outstanding, so I tried asking. We're the same age." His confidence seeped from every word and gesture, as if saying no one under heaven could touch him.

"But it seems I expected too much. Sword songs are the most luxurious entertainment among the world's arts. How could I ask about something that only applies to royalty? I am sorry and ashamed."

He smiled slightly and turned around with his hands behind his back.

"Everyone return. It was a pleasant meeting."

---

"How was the crown prince?" Shin So-bin thrust her small face forward.

All of Radiant Demon Wing were waiting. They had set up a banquet at the main training ground.

"Well. We received the order to leave too quickly," Yeon-shin said.

The fragrant smell of the feast stimulated even his dantian. His hunger had increased after ceremonies absent from his life.

Shaoxing wine. Hyeon Won-chang had rushed to Xiangyang's First Tavern to obtain it -- precious wine from distant Zhejiang. A colleague's heart and wine taste were separate matters. Taking a sip along with Ma Jin's congratulations, Yeon-shin furrowed his brow.

The seniors giggled. Even Shin So-bin looked at him like a child.

"Lightning Flash is blue-level."

"Incredibly short time."

"Aren't his achievements substantial? The Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword, the Seventh Apostle's eye..."

Even Desolate Sect's masters revered by all under heaven were like commoners when drinking.

Yeon-shin's mind wandered from the celebration.

*He said sword song. Playing music with sword sounds.*

He had heard of sound techniques in the murim. A story of an internal force master facing a hundred people by playing a seven-stringed zither, bursting enemies' eardrums by plucking strings. The principle was not difficult to guess.

*True energy.*

Internal force, carried on the air. The question was effectiveness. That was where the difference between supreme and third-rate divided.

*Maybe I can create sound techniques with a sword.*

If successful, it would become easier to handle multiple enemies.

"Drink!"

"The side dishes here are gone!"

The banquet was no longer about him. Everyone enjoyed themselves, and that made it more comfortable. Yeon-shin began conceiving sound techniques while tolerating Hyeon Won-chang's drunken behaviour.

A boisterous day set. The moonlit night was cosy.

---

The next morning. Promotion compensation.

The armory of Desolate Sect's forge -- world's first in metalworking.

"Choose quickly. They're all famous swords."

The dwarf clan man came up to Yeon-shin's waist. Thick beard, short limbs, arms of tremendous thickness. Sturdier in visible muscle than Yeon-shin himself.

"But wouldn't there be one that fits my hand?"

"They were made to fit all people under heaven. Before any man blames the weapon, he should look back at his own martial arts first."

Cheong Myeong walked beside him through the armory. All weapons looked extraordinary.

"Still need to be careful," Cheong Myeong said. "It's your one promotion reward. They say you can get something else when becoming black-level."

"Hey, Blue Eyes Demon Sword," the dwarf man said. "Is this youngster really blue-level? I can't believe it."

"Can't you tell by his clothes?" Cheong Myeong's tone sharpened. The dwarf closed his mouth.

Yeon-shin stopped.

A sword hanging on the display. Both sword wave and scabbard were pure white. It would contrast with the pitch-black Desolate Sword at his waist.

"How about that one?"

"Northern Light. It's a good one."

After getting permission, Yeon-shin grabbed the sword. He felt the hilt's sensation for a moment, then drew the blade.

Wave patterns flickered on the dazzlingly pure white steel. He infused true energy with his right hand.

Top grade. The unobstructed energy extended out and played on the blade like water finding its course. He brought the blade close and stroked it with his left hand. Pale blue lightning flashed in his eyes.

"With this level I could perform music with sword sounds."

"You?" The dwarf man snickered.

"Quiet," Cheong Myeong said.

Yeon-shin had already closed his eyes. Without swinging it, he knew -- how to move the sword, how to ride the air with slightly different sword paths. A realm separate from proficiency. Though it would take time to familiarize with it like the Desolate Sword, he instantly felt what kind of sword Northern Light was.

Innate sense.

From quite far away, unfamiliar energy waves approached. Noble. As if about to rise into the sky.

"Didn't I tell you to choose quickly?" the dwarf man frowned. "The crown prince is coming."

*Not coincidence.*

Royal processions had extreme aspects. Unless in seclusion or on a secret journey, anyone in Desolate Sect could know. Yeon-shin had chosen this time deliberately -- wondering if he could gain even a little inspiration from imperial martial arts.

"Crown prince. He's been going around often lately?"

"They say martial arts became his attachment after the crown princess's passing," the dwarf man said. "Trying to soothe his heart with the sword after losing his mother."

"His mother...?" Yeon-shin repeated.

*The door rattled.*

He examined Northern Light for a moment. Then asked Cheong Myeong as if it were not important:

"When gaining the crown prince's favour, would it help with main fortress personnel matters? For example, gaining support when challenging for the Divine Sword Squad Leader position?"

"Well, it would be better than having no friendship. It's not a position reached by martial power alone."

Then it was good.

He stepped out of the armory.

True energy surged into Northern Light. The blade emitted strange vibrating sounds -- a sound like a newborn flood dragon crying. Then a serpent's groan breaking its shell gradually transformed into something deeper.

A dragon's growl.

The sword's song spread straight out. A resonance as if yearning for something far away, approaching to the point of being sorrowful. The cry buried even the dwarf man's shocked voice.

---

The crown prince heard it.

He had been walking toward the armory, muttering to himself about sword songs -- the art his father once demonstrated with a blade whenever he composed his mind. At some point, they could no longer be seen. The crown princess's death had drained something vital from the heir apparent, and the music had died with it.

"Sword songs," he said softly. "Who else would cultivate such a luxurious hobby."

Having lost his mother early, his soliloquies had increased. Those serving him knew well enough not to react.

Then, under the falling twilight, he saw a youth holding down a sword while making blue robes flutter over blue martial clothes.

Lightning Flash.

The young swordsman whose activities the Desolate Sect Lord he admired received reports about daily.

An anecdote arose in the crown prince's mind. In the Spring and Autumn period, a music master had called someone "knowing the sound" -- a close friend who understood his performance better than anyone.

The vibrating sounds spreading from the youth's sword reached him. His steps quickened.

And he saw. It felt like his ears opened of their own accord.

A serpent's groan, breaking its shell, transforming into a dragon's growl. A resonance yearning for something distant, approaching to the point of sorrow. The sword song that seemed to rise from a dragon's throat rewound the crown prince's old memories -- his father in times still full of vitality, the sword's song drifting through the evening gardens of the palace.

He rested his heart in those distant memories.

"To think there was one who would make me know the sound..." he whispered.

The twilight deepened around them both, and the sword sang on.
